name: Asana
description: Project management platform for teams. Track tasks, set deadlines, assign work, and visualise progress across projects.

name: Cal.com
description: Open-source Calendly alternative. Scheduling infrastructure for individuals and teams. Self-host or use the cloud version. Integrates with Google, Outlook, Zoom.

name: Excalidraw
description: Virtual whiteboard for sketching diagrams with a hand-drawn feel. Collaborative, real-time, and privacy-first. Perfect for system diagrams and brainstorming.

name: Notion
description: All-in-one workspace combining notes, databases, wikis, and project boards. Highly customisable for individuals and teams.

name: Obsidian
description: Local-first knowledge base and note-taking app built on Markdown files. Graph view, plugins, and bidirectional links make it the ultimate second brain tool.

name: Slack
description: Channel-based team messaging with integrations for hundreds of tools. Organise conversations, share files, and connect your workflow.

name: Todoist
description: Clean, powerful task manager available on every platform. Natural language input, recurring tasks, project boards, and productivity tracking built in.

name: Trello
description: Visual project management with Kanban boards. Drag cards through columns to track tasks and workflows at a glance.

name: Linear
description: Blazing-fast issue tracker built for modern software teams. Keyboard-first, opinionated, and beloved by devs who've used Jira.

name: Cron
description: Next-generation calendar app for professionals. Clean UI, keyboard shortcuts, scheduling links, and team availability at a glance.

name: Reclaim.ai
description: AI scheduling assistant that automatically defends time for your habits, tasks, and breaks. Integrates with Google Calendar.

name: Raindrop.io
description: Beautiful bookmark manager. Save links from any device, organize into collections, add highlights and notes, and find anything instantly.

name: Readwise
description: Resurface your highlights from Kindle, Instapaper, and web articles daily. Spaced repetition for things you've read.

name: Logseq
description: Open-source outliner and knowledge base. Block-based note-taking with bi-directional links, local storage by default.
